untranslatable_reason: Pattern definition requires identifying 'expanding price oscillations with lower lows and higher highs' which cannot be directly expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. This requires multi-bar pattern recognition and trend analysis beyond simple technical indicator values. Entry/exit conditions marked AUTHOR_SILENT for both sides. Donchian channel breakouts used as proxy for volatility expansion characteristic of broadening bottoms, but true pattern detection would require custom pattern recognition logic.
translation_notes: Broadening Bottom is a complex multi-bar chart pattern characterized by expanding volatility. The entry conditions reference a downtrend leading to formation start, and expanding oscillations. Using Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) as a technical proxy for detecting the expanding price range characteristic. Actual pattern detection would require identifying: (1) preceding downtrend, (2) sequence of lower lows and higher highs, and (3) symmetry/expansion metrics. Success rates from source: 62% bull, 34% bear; average rise 27%, decline 15%. Stop loss set conservatively at 5% given mixed success rates.
